title: Justicia biokoensis
intro: Justicia biokoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to the island of Bioko in Equatorial Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia biokoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Justicia biokoensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Justicia biokoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to the island of Bioko, located off the coast of Equatorial Guinea. The classification of Justicia biokoensis places it within the genus Justicia and the order Lamiales. Further classification includes its family, Acanthaceae, and the subclass Asteridae. This classification suggests that Justicia biokoensis shares characteristics with other species in the same family and order.
